ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for prata bomb (cheese). Mapped to flatbread as the closest defensible parent class; prata is a pan-fried laminated flatbread of South Asian origin.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for prata bomb. A0CKP (flatbread, pan-fried) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Cheese-stuffed variant is documented in notes.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for prata bomb or roti prata in the clinical food hierarchy as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One prata bomb (cheese) typically weighs approximately 120 g and is served as a single piece at mamak stalls, often accompanied by dhal or curry sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Prata Bomb (Cheese) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Prata Bomb (Cheese), CR ID F05020094. All per-100g macros, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from ingredient composition (ghee + processed cheese + margarine) using USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles as a secondary reference, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database — Roti / flatbread analogous entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prata bomb (cheese) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entries: white wheat flatbread / roti (GI 62–80, median ~70) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The cheese and sugar filling slightly elevates the effective GI relative to plain roti. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × available carbohydrate per serving (25.11 g/100g × 120 g serving = 30.13 g) ÷ 100 = 21.1 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 566 mg/100g; a single 120 g prata bomb delivers approximately 679 mg sodium — more than 45% of the 1500 mg daily target for hypertensive individuals — with negligible potassium (not reported) and minimal fibre (0.69 g/100g) to offset vascular load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit prata bomb (cheese) to occasional consumption only, request reduced cheese and less ghee/margarine during preparation, and avoid pairing with high-sodium curry sauces to keep total meal sodium below 800 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) combined with 25.1 g/100g carbohydrate, 8.49 g/100g added sugar, and only 0.69 g/100g dietary fibre produces a glycemic load of ~21 per serving — well above the low-GL threshold of 10 — causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit prata bomb (cheese); if consumed, restrict to half a piece (≈60 g), pair with a high-protein curry or dhal to blunt glucose absorption, and monitor postprandial blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100g (from ghee and processed cheese) and trans fat ~0.3 g/100g (from margarine) together exceed cardiovascular risk thresholds; sodium at 566 mg/100g further elevates blood pressure risk, compounding atherosclerotic burden.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat prata bomb (cheese) as an occasional indulgence only, request preparation with minimal ghee and no margarine, limit portion to 60 g, and avoid pairing with coconut-based or high-fat gravies to reduce saturated fat and trans fat intake per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat ~0.3 g/100g from margarine directly activates pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; saturated fat ~7.5 g/100g from ghee and processed cheese exceeds the RA threshold; high-temperature pan-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid prata bomb (cheese) due to its trans fat and high saturated fat content; if consumed, request butter-free and margarine-free preparation and limit to a small portion paired with anti-inflammatory accompaniments such as turmeric-based curry.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prata bomb (cheese) is a thick, indulgent variant of the classic Singaporean mamak roti prata — a pan-fried laminated flatbread of South Indian origin that has become a beloved staple at hawker centres and mamak stalls across Singapore. Unlike the standard plain prata, the prata bomb is made with a substantially thicker dough that is folded and stuffed generously with processed cheese, margarine, and sugar before being cooked on a well-oiled flat griddle at high heat. The result is a crispy, golden-brown exterior with a soft, layered, slightly chewy interior that oozes melted cheese and carries a sweet-savoury flavour profile. The cheese filling contributes a creamy, salty richness while the sugar adds a mild sweetness that contrasts with the savoury ghee-coated dough. Nutritionally, prata bomb (cheese) is energy-dense at 250 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 14.31 g/100g — predominantly from ghee and processed cheese — and a moderate carbohydrate load of 25.11 g/100g derived from refined white flour. Sugar at 8.49 g/100g and sodium at 566 mg/100g are both clinically significant. Dietary fibre is very low at 0.69 g/100g, providing no meaningful glycemic buffering. The estimated glycemic index of approximately 70 places this food in the high-GI category, with a glycemic load of approximately 21 per standard 120 g serving. Clinically, prata bomb (cheese) poses elevated risk for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis due to its combination of high sodium, high saturated fat, trans fat from margarine, added sugar, and refined carbohydrate. It is typically consumed at breakfast or as a late-night snack and is commonly paired with dhal, fish curry, or chicken curry. It is halal-certified at most mamak establishments and is suitable for lacto-vegetarians.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F05020094) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from ingredient composition using USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles as a secondary reference. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Ontology anchors are defensible approximations with documented gap notes.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (7.5 g/100g) and trans fat (0.3 g/100g) are estimated from ingredient composition — ghee, processed cheese, and margarine — using USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles (SR Legacy), as HPB does not report these fractions separately for this item. These values should be replaced with direct analytical data when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this specific prata bomb (cheese) preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white wheat flatbread / roti entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for prata bomb (cheese) exists in an approved source. 4. Serving size of 120 g is a typical mamak-stall portion estimate; actual weight may vary by vendor. 5. The description in the input JSON states 'stuffed with margarine and sugar' — the cheese filling is confirmed by the food name and HPB record; margarine and sugar are co-ingredients consistent with standard prata bomb preparation.
